jokes.text: Martin doing impressions: 'I'm Quentin Tarantino... I'm John Malkovich... I'm Tommy Lee Jones... Also, I'm George W. Bush.'
jokes.setup: Character doing celebrity impressions for talent show audition
jokes.punchline: Randomly adding 'Also, I'm George W. Bush' at the end with no context
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with random Bush addition, but fairly simple structure

jokes.text: Leslie: 'Who are any of those people?'
jokes.setup: Martin just did impressions of famous celebrities
jokes.punchline: Leslie genuinely doesn't recognize any major Hollywood figures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her obliviousness to pop culture is well-established and this delivers economically

jokes.text: Leslie's talking head about completion of tasks: 'I hate tasks that are uncompleted. I live for the check marks in columns indicating the completion of tasks.'
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her motivation
jokes.punchline: Obsessive specificity about check marks in columns
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Leslie's type-A personality

jokes.text: Perd: 'This is a slam poem that I wrote. And I am speaking the slam poem to you right now with my mouth.'
jokes.setup: Perd introducing his slam poetry performance
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious that he's speaking with his mouth
jokes.explanation: Classic Perd - stating the obvious in the most redundant way possible. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Character reaction: 'I cannot tell the difference between what's supposed to be slam poetry and what's just him talking.'
jokes.setup: Perd's confusing slam poetry performance
jokes.punchline: Someone pointing out it's indistinguishable from his regular speech
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that highlights Perd's verbal quirks

jokes.text: Perd: 'My second slam poem is called "My Second Slam Poem by Perd Hapley," by Perd Hapley.'
jokes.setup: Perd introducing his second poem
jokes.punchline: Redundantly crediting himself twice in the title announcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent Perd moment with redundant self-attribution that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: Andy asking about vitamins: 'Is that the Fred one or the Barney one?'
jokes.setup: Leslie tells Andy to take vitamin C
jokes.punchline: Andy confuses vitamins with Flintstones characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - childlike confusion delivered with economy and character consistency

jokes.text: Leslie: 'We used to crown Miss Influenza every year. The CDC called the pageant "ethically reprehensible".'
jokes.setup: Pawnee's history with disease
jokes.punchline: They had a beauty pageant celebrating the flu that the CDC condemned
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist world-building - Miss Influenza pageant is hilariously specific and wrong

jokes.text: Leslie to Larry in quarantine tent: 'The tent is your home now, Larry. We already forwarded your mail.'
jokes.setup: Larry is quarantined for being sick
jokes.punchline: They've already forwarded his mail, suggesting permanent relocation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing Leslie's extreme measures and Larry's treatment

jokes.text: Chipp McCapp fan explanation: 'We are huge Chippmunks. That's what his fans call themselves. I don't know why.'
jokes.setup: Explaining Chipp McCapp's fanbase nickname
jokes.punchline: Admitting they don't understand their own fandom name
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about arbitrary fan culture naming

jokes.text: Tom's restaurant philosophy: 'People that convince other people to buy expensive stuff they don't need. Those are the real heroes.'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he wants to hire sommeliers
jokes.punchline: Calling manipulative salespeople 'real heroes'
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his shallow entrepreneurial mindset

jokes.text: Craig: 'I studied botany for three years at Purdue University. Go, Boilermakers!'
jokes.setup: Craig claiming wine expertise
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic school spirit interrupting his serious credentials
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Craig's intensity and random enthusiasm

jokes.text: Tom: 'I started with two cool people and I ended up with a bunch of grandpas.'
jokes.setup: Tom's wine trip party growing beyond his control
jokes.punchline: Describing his Parks department colleagues as 'grandpas'
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his image consciousness and age prejudice

jokes.text: Tom to group: 'You're already doing it. I begged you.'
jokes.setup: Tom asking how they would embarrass him
jokes.punchline: They're already embarrassing him just by asking the question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's exasperation with immediate confirmation

jokes.text: Leslie's wish list item: 'Bob Dylan's friends'
jokes.setup: Looking at Leslie's concert performer wish list
jokes.punchline: Not Bob Dylan himself, but his friends
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic showing Leslie's indirect approach to getting Dylan

jokes.text: Land Ho album title: 'We'll Never Reunite'
jokes.setup: Explaining why Land Ho won't reunite
jokes.punchline: Their last album was literally titled about never reuniting
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor with very specific and definitive album title

jokes.text: Leslie to Larry: 'No! Back in your tent, sicky.'
jokes.setup: Larry appears when Leslie needs help
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately banishing him back to quarantine
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier quarantine joke with Leslie's harsh treatment of Larry

jokes.text: Leslie: 'Oh, no. Damn it, Larry!' Andy: 'Sorry.'
jokes.setup: Someone does something wrong
jokes.punchline: Leslie blames Larry but Andy apologizes, suggesting he's the actual culprit
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy in subverting the Larry blame running gag

jokes.text: Tom's introduction: 'Tom Haverford. Owner, operator, and global ambassador for Tom's Bistro'
jokes.setup: Tom introducing himself professionally
jokes.punchline: 'Global ambassador' for a local restaurant that doesn't exist yet
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his grandiose self-presentation

jokes.text: Tom: 'He's basically the Bruno Mars of Indiana amateur wine tasting.'
jokes.setup: Tom describing Xander's expertise
jokes.punchline: Comparing wine tasting to Bruno Mars in hyper-specific regional context
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comparison that's very specific to Tom's pop culture references

jokes.text: Xander: 'I pour saline water through my nose to clear the mucus from my smell centers.'
jokes.setup: Xander preparing for wine tasting
jokes.punchline: Gross medical procedure described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from pretentious wine culture practices

jokes.text: Tom's reaction: 'That is the most beautiful thing I've ever heard.'
jokes.setup: Xander describing gross nose-clearing procedure
jokes.punchline: Tom finding it beautiful instead of disgusting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom reaction showing his shallow obsession with expertise performance

jokes.text: April: 'I wanted to make fun of stupid people while I get drunk. My two true passions.'
jokes.setup: April wanting to enter wine competition
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of wanting to mock people and drink
jokes.explanation: Excellent April moment - perfectly captures her cynical honesty and priorities

jokes.text: April claiming she studied at 'The Wine Academy... In Bordeaux'
jokes.setup: April asked about her wine education
jokes.punchline: Confidently making up prestigious fake credentials
jokes.explanation: Good April moment showing her ability to lie convincingly with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Craig: 'My name is Craig Middlebrooks, and this is my debit rewards card.'
jokes.setup: Craig trying to prove his wine credentials
jokes.punchline: Offering his debit card as proof of expertise
jokes.explanation: Good Craig moment showing his intense but misguided approach

jokes.text: Ben to Ron: 'Did you not hear me say there was no need to discuss it with me?'
jokes.setup: Ben saying Ron doesn't need to discuss personal issues with him
jokes.punchline: Ron continuing to discuss them anyway, forcing Ben to repeat himself
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Ben's discomfort with personal sharing

jokes.text: Ron: 'Put some alcohol in your mouth to block the words from coming out.'
jokes.setup: Ben continuing to overshare about family problems
jokes.punchline: Ron suggesting alcohol as a conversation stopper
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron line - perfectly delivers his directness and discomfort with emotions

jokes.text: Ron: 'I don't drink alcohol from that portion of the color spectrum.'
jokes.setup: Offered blueberry wine
jokes.punchline: Describing wine colors as a 'spectrum' to avoid saying he doesn't like fruity drinks
jokes.explanation: Great Ron moment using technical language to maintain masculine dignity

jokes.text: Andy: 'I do it all the time' about puking in his car
jokes.setup: Andy offering comfort about potentially puking in his car
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting he regularly pukes in his own car
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out Andy moment showing his low standards and oversharing

jokes.text: Andy about first aid: 'Or karate. That's not first aid. It is if you do it right.'
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to know first aid
jokes.punchline: Confusing first aid with karate, then doubling down on the logic
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic showing his confident absurd reasoning

jokes.text: Leslie: 'The Mariah-needs-to-sing-tonight stuff.'
jokes.setup: Leslie asking pharmacist for strong flu medicine
jokes.punchline: Referencing Mariah Carey's diva demands for performance medication
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that shows Leslie's work-obsessed mentality

jokes.text: Pharmacist: 'Could be aseptic meningitis... It'd mean you have, like, an hour to live... It's probably not that. Sorry, it's my first day.'
jokes.setup: Pharmacist diagnosing Leslie's symptoms
jokes.punchline: Suggesting fatal illness then casually admitting inexperience
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with darkly comic medical incompetence

jokes.text: Andy with giant candy: 'This counts as one.'
jokes.setup: Leslie said Andy could buy two candies
jokes.punchline: Andy holding enormous candy claiming it counts as one piece
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Andy's childlike rule-bending logic

jokes.text: Ron: 'You may vent for as long as it takes me to drink this bottle of wine.'
jokes.setup: Ben asking if he can vent about personal problems
jokes.punchline: Ron setting a drinking-based time limit on emotional sharing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron line perfectly capturing his tolerance for emotions and love of alcohol

jokes.text: Ben: 'You're a terrible person to discuss personal problems with.' Ron: 'Thank you, friend. That really means a lot to me.'
jokes.setup: Ben criticizing Ron's emotional support skills
jokes.punchline: Ron taking the insult as a sincere compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Ron genuinely appreciates being seen as emotionally unavailable

jokes.text: April wine tasting: 'It's like a Charlie Brown Halloween special... so much pumpkin'
jokes.setup: April describing wine flavors professionally
jokes.punchline: Comparing wine to cartoon Halloween special
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist comparison that's unexpectedly specific and visual

jokes.text: April: 'I'm getting notes of dried robin's blood, old, dirty cashews from your grandfather's basement, and just a hint of a robot's bathwater.'
jokes.setup: April continuing her wine description
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and gross flavor descriptions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - each flavor note gets more specific and disgusting in hilarious ways

jokes.text: Craig wine description: 'Some place beautiful and warm and amazing where everyone is in great shape and the night sky is full of stars!'
jokes.setup: Craig attempting sophisticated wine description
jokes.punchline: Devolving into romantic vacation brochure language
jokes.explanation: Good Craig moment showing his intense but unfocused wine analysis

jokes.text: April: 'This comes from your mother's butt. Possibly your father's butt... This comes from John McEnroe's butt from the year 3055.'
jokes.setup: April's wine descriptions getting more absurd
jokes.punchline: Time-traveling celebrity butt wine from the future
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation combining scatological humor with time travel and random celebrity

jokes.text: Andy: 'I made all the little coffee pods they had.'
jokes.setup: Andy waiting in reception area
jokes.punchline: Making coffee with every single pod available
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his tendency to overdo simple tasks

jokes.text: Ben on phone: 'Hey, honey. Hey. Uh, it's your me. It's wife. It's Leslie. Knope.'
jokes.setup: Ben trying to talk on phone while drunk
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled self-identification
jokes.explanation: Good drunk comedy showing Ben's confusion and Leslie's shocked reaction

jokes.text: Ben's drunk synagogue story: 'Just like the time they couldn't decide who gets the dog, so they just dropped it off at the local synagogue. I was, like, Say what?'
jokes.setup: Ben drunkenly continuing family stories
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate dog disposal method
jokes.explanation: Decent drunk Ben moment - absurd family dysfunction that gets darker the more he talks

jokes.text: Ben: 'I was just saying to Ron that my dog's Jewish.'
jokes.setup: Ben trying to make conversation while drunk
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about his dog's religion
jokes.explanation: Good absurd drunk logic combining earlier family story in confused way

jokes.text: Leslie: 'It's as dead as Stanley Kubrick. I miss them both so much, Ron.'
jokes.setup: Leslie's phone died
jokes.punchline: Comparing dead phone to dead director and mourning both equally
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with unexpected emotional attachment to Stanley Kubrick

jokes.text: Tom: 'You know I picked up a little French-Canadian when I dated that Montreal Expo.'
jokes.setup: Tom hearing French being spoken
jokes.punchline: Claiming to speak French-Canadian from dating a baseball team member
jokes.explanation: Good Tom boast about imaginary sophisticated dating history

jokes.text: French restaurateur: 'That is everyone's idea. That is why we have this, how you say, thing.'
jokes.setup: Tom claiming hiring sommeliers was his idea
jokes.punchline: French guy dismissing it as obvious while pretending not to know word 'thing'
jokes.explanation: Good pretentious character moment with fake language barrier

jokes.text: Tom: 'Okay, don't act like you don't know the word "thing."'
jokes.setup: French restaurateur pretending not to know simple English
jokes.punchline: Tom calling out the obvious fake language barrier
jokes.explanation: Good callback moment with Tom's reasonable frustration

jokes.text: April wine recommendation: 'Something bold and dry, like David Sedaris.'
jokes.setup: April recommending wine for steak
jokes.punchline: Describing wine using personality traits of a humor writer
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist comparison using literary figure's personality as wine descriptor

jokes.text: April: 'You know in the movies, when the cops show up and find lipstick on a wine glass next to a dead body? This is that wine.'
jokes.setup: April describing wine characteristics
jokes.punchline: Comparing wine to evidence at crime scenes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with very specific cinematic reference that's unexpectedly fitting

jokes.text: Chipp: 'You're the real superstars.' followed immediately by demanding LunchStacks
jokes.setup: Chipp giving fans humble, gracious response
jokes.punchline: Immediately being demanding and rude to staff
jokes.explanation: Good character irony showing fake public persona vs. real personality

jokes.text: Chipp to his father: 'Cheese on top, or no one gets paid... You dick. I want ham.'
jokes.setup: Chipp being demanding about food preparation
jokes.punchline: Calling his own father a dick over sandwich ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing entitled celebrity behavior toward family

jokes.text: Chipp's father: 'Pick them up, Skipp.' after being humiliated
jokes.setup: Chipp throwing food and berating his father
jokes.punchline: Father sadly accepting abuse and cleaning up
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable cringe moment that works but is more sad than funny

jokes.text: April: 'I think we can agree that all wine tastes the same. And if you spend more than $5 on wine, you are very stupid.'
jokes.setup: April winning sommelier certification
jokes.punchline: Immediately denouncing the entire premise of wine expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - winning by being completely anti-wine while surrounded by wine snobs

jokes.text: April's acceptance speech: 'Norbit, uh, Pluto Nash, all the Klumps.'
jokes.setup: April giving acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: Thanking Eddie Murphy's worst movie characters instead of people
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with very specific bad movie references as inspiration

jokes.text: Tom salary negotiation: 'I'll double whatever he's paying you... I will pay you one-tenth of whatever he's paying you.'
jokes.setup: Tom trying to outbid for the sommelier
jokes.punchline: Immediately backing down to one-tenth when hearing the salary
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and reversal showing Tom's financial reality vs. his ambitions

jokes.text: Donna's wine shortage: 'Aw, man, this wine bottle is all out of the wine'
jokes.setup: Donna drinking heavily at vineyard
jokes.punchline: Childlike way of expressing empty bottle
jokes.explanation: Decent Donna drunk moment with childish phrasing that fits her direct personality

jokes.text: Ron Dunn: 'All depends on whether your conception of time is linear or circular.'
jokes.setup: Andy asking where Ron Dunn came from
jokes.punchline: Philosophical response to simple question about location
jokes.explanation: Good hippie character moment with unnecessary philosophical complexity

jokes.text: Chipp: 'They sure ain't buying toothpaste. Bunch of dirty hicks.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Pawnee residents buying albums
jokes.punchline: Insulting his own fanbase as dirty hicks
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chipp's contempt for his audience

jokes.text: Chipp shooting paintball at his father: 'Good shot, son. I'm proud of you.'
jokes.setup: Chipp demonstrating his spoiled behavior
jokes.punchline: Father praising son for shooting him
jokes.explanation: Dark cringe humor showing dysfunctional family dynamic

jokes.text: Leslie: 'When you shook my hand earlier, there was pee on my palms.'
jokes.setup: Leslie insulting Chipp as she leaves
jokes.punchline: Admitting to having pee on her hands during their introduction
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out escalation that makes Leslie's insult even weirder

jokes.text: Andy: 'You get pee-palm, too? Every time!'
jokes.setup: Leslie revealing her pee hands
jokes.punchline: Andy treating it as a common, relatable experience
jokes.explanation: Great Andy moment treating gross situation as totally normal occurrence

jokes.text: Andy's pregnancy advice: 'Just because your family is getting bigger does not mean that you can't handle it.'
jokes.setup: Andy giving Leslie life advice
jokes.punchline: Accidentally giving perfect pregnancy advice while thinking she's getting a dog
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with Andy accidentally being profound while completely wrong

jokes.text: Andy: 'I thought you were getting a dog.' Leslie: 'Why did you go like this?' Andy: 'That's a sign for petting a dog that's sitting on your lap.'
jokes.setup: Revealing Andy's misunderstanding about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Andy explaining his dog-petting gesture interpretation
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy with Andy's innocent misinterpretation of pregnancy gestures

jokes.text: Waitress: 'Put it in a doggy bag.' Andy: 'She's gettin' a dog!'
jokes.setup: Waitress offering doggy bag for leftovers
jokes.punchline: Andy excitedly thinking this confirms Leslie's getting a dog
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Andy finding confirmation in unrelated doggy bag reference

jokes.text: Ron Dunn: 'The Wamapoke called it "mattapiqua." They believed it had mystical powers.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Ben's blueberry wine consumption
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Native American backstory for simple wine observation
jokes.explanation: Good hippie character moment with unnecessary historical context

jokes.text: Ron Dunn role-play: 'I'll be your father. And, Ron, you take the role of Ben's anger.'
jokes.setup: Ron Dunn suggesting therapeutic role-play
jokes.punchline: Assigning Ron Swanson to play the abstract concept of anger
jokes.explanation: Great absurd therapy concept with Ron Swanson as personified anger

jokes.text: Ron Swanson: 'Well, goodnight, everyone. I'm going to go home and feel totally fine.'
jokes.setup: Therapeutic role-play beginning
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately leaving rather than participate in emotions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - immediate emotional avoidance with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Craig: 'I'm going to go home, put on a Macy Gray album, and cry in the mirror!'
jokes.setup: Craig not getting the sommelier job
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic and specific emotional breakdown plan
jokes.explanation: Good Craig moment with hyper-specific dramatic emotional response

jokes.text: Craig: 'I'll bring it down 1,000 notches if I have to!'
jokes.setup: Tom saying Craig needs to tone down his intensity
jokes.punchline: Craig responding with extreme intensity about being less intense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig paradox - being extremely intense about being less intense

jokes.text: Andy about SATs: 'Same way I got a perfect score on the SATs. Broken Scantron machine.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his strategy for convincing Scott Tanner
jokes.punchline: Admitting his perfect SAT score was due to malfunctioning equipment
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his accidental success through incompetence

jokes.text: Johnny Karate theme song performance with Scott Tanner
jokes.setup: Andy performing children's song to connect with Scott
jokes.punchline: Professional musician joining in ridiculous karate chop song
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy sequence that sets up the band reunion through music bonding

jokes.text: Johnny Karate song performance with Scott joining in
jokes.setup: Andy performing children's karate song
jokes.punchline: Professional musician getting drawn into ridiculous kids' song
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment that shows Andy's ability to connect with people through silly performance

jokes.text: Craig as sommelier: Customer orders red wine with fish and ice cubes, plus dog bowl service with silly straws
jokes.setup: Tom testing Craig's sommelier skills with difficult customers
jokes.punchline: Customers ordering increasingly ridiculous wine combinations to test him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd customer demands testing Craig's professionalism

jokes.text: Ben: 'Because it doesn't exist because that's nonsense because you're an adult.'
jokes.setup: Asked about his spirit animal
jokes.punchline: Increasingly emphatic rejection of the concept
jokes.explanation: Good Ben moment showing his rational skepticism with mounting frustration

jokes.text: Ron Dunn: 'Baby snow owl.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Ben's spirit animal
jokes.punchline: Immediate, confident assignment of very specific and cute spirit animal
jokes.explanation: Great economy in hippie character moment with unexpectedly specific and adorable choice

jokes.text: Andy about dog care: 'You're gonna need a cage, a water bowl... Tick medicine.'
jokes.setup: Andy giving Leslie advice about her 'pregnancy'
jokes.punchline: Continuing to give dog care advice while thinking she's having a baby
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the dog misunderstanding with practical but wrong advice

jokes.text: Ron Dunn about his car: 'This thing runs on vegetable oil and bat guano. Actually cleans the air as it drives.'
jokes.setup: Ron Dunn describing his transportation
jokes.punchline: Impossibly eco-friendly car that improves the environment while driving
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist hippie technology that perfectly fits Ron Dunn's character

jokes.text: Ben: 'Yeah, I don't feel any better. I'm still angry. Damn it.'
jokes.setup: After elaborate spiritual healing ceremony
jokes.punchline: Ben admitting the therapy didn't work at all
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment deflating the hippie therapy with Ben's honesty

jokes.text: Ron Swanson's family perspective: 'It's always nice to see the kids at the end of a long day. Really puts things in perspective'
jokes.setup: Ben realizing he wants to start a family
jokes.punchline: Ron offering rare emotional wisdom about family priorities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron's softer side about family while setting up Ben's realization
